channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Court rebuffs plea from domestic workers for better pay and respect
item.description: They're often paid low wages and lack job protections. A petition to the country's supreme court to support their demands did not see success â and th...

item.title: 6 GOP reps defy Trump to block Canadian tariffs. And, student loan defaults rise
item.description: Six House Republicans joined Democrats to successfully block Trump's Canadian tariffs. And, roughly a million borrowers defaulted on their federal stu...

item.title: U.S. cross-country star Jessie Diggins claims bronze, medaling in third straight Olympics
item.description: She's done it again. Jessie Diggins, age 34 and skiing in her final Olympics, captured a bronze medal for the U.S. at the Milan Cortina Games. She bat...

item.title: Spy agency says Kim Jong Un's daughter is close to be North Korea's future leader
item.description: Seoul's assessment comes as North Korea is preparing to hold its biggest political conference later this month, where Kim is expected to outline his m...

item.title: Using GLP-1s to maintain a normal weight? There are benefits and risks
item.description: Drugs like Zepbound and Wegovy are intended for people who are overweight. Some patients are using them after bariatric surgery to keep pounds from cr...

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: February 12, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? These are the mortgage interest rates to know right now.

item.title: Port city in ruins as storm rips through Madagascar and on towards Mozambique
item.description: More than half the population is affected and most infrastructure destroyed in Toamasina, Madagascar's main port.

item.title: Two US Navy ships collide near South America
item.description: Two people involved in the operation suffered minor injuries and are in stable condition, the US military said.

item.title: Prince Harry thanks bereaved families taking on social media firms
item.description: The Duke of Sussex addressed parents claiming Instagram and YouTube damage young people's mental health.

item.title: Constance Zimmer on portraying powerful characters and the future of "The Lincoln Lawyer"
item.description: Constance Zimmer, who stars in the new season of "The Lincoln Lawyer," talks about how she channels her powerful character in the series and why it's ...

item.title: Minnesota immigration enforcement surge is ending, Trump border tsar says
item.description: Two US citizens were killed in Minneapolis during the crackdown dubbed "Operation Metro Surge".

item.title: Polls close in first election since Gen Z protests ousted Bangladesh leader
item.description: Voters are hoping for a return to democracy after 15 years of authoritarian rule under Sheikh Hasina.

item.title: Watch Live: Minnesota officials testify at Senate hearing on immigration
item.description: Four Minnesota officials and the leaders of three major immigration agencies are testifying before the Senate Homeland Security Committee on Thursday.

item.title: Border czar announces Minnesota immigration operation surge is ending
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan announced Thursday that Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota is concluding, with a drawdown of federal immigration officers set to...

item.title: Black gloves found in Nancy Guthrie search, sources say
item.description: The gloves will be tested for DNA as the search for Nancy Guthrie continues.

item.title: Pentagon let CBP use anti-drone laser before FAA closed El Paso airspace, AP sources say
item.description: The agency says it will ground all flights to and from the airport for âspecial security reasons.â

item.title: Few leads in Guthrie investigation after 11 days, yet cases often break unexpectedly
FBI combs desert terrain for clues in Nancy Guthrieâs disappearance
